title: Centaurea nana
intro: Centaurea nana, also known as dwarf cornflower, is a low-growing, perennial plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its small, delicate flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it a useful remedy for treating various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Centaurea nana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Centaurea nana is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory problems, as well as to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Centaurea nana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Centaurea, which comprises a large group of thistle-like plants. Within the genus, Centaurea nana is further classified as a member of the subgenus Cacaliastrum. The species is native to the Mediterranean region and is often cultivated for its small, delicate flowers and compact growth habit. It is commonly known as dwarf cornflower or rock cornflower due to its small stature and bright blue flowers.
